dsl_rule.translation_notes: Mapped 'n-period highest high/lowest low channel' to Donchian Channel (dc_upper/dc_lower) with lookback_period=55. Used dc_middle as channel centerline for position detection. Exit conditions trigger on price reversal back inside channel (crossing dc_middle) or opposite boundary breach. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal specifies both long (above highest) and short (below lowest) breakout entries. Target regime set to TRENDING modes per signal specification.

backtest_rule.reason: Mapped 'n-period highest high/lowest low channel' to Donchian Channel (dc_upper/dc_lower) with lookback_period=55. Used dc_middle as channel centerline for position detection. Exit conditions trigger on price reversal back inside channel (crossing dc_middle) or opposite boundary breach. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal specifies both long (above highest) and short (below lowest) breakout entries. Target regime set to TRENDING modes per signal specification.

source_rule_text: A trader should adjust the lookback period of a channel breakout indicator based on the trend magnitude they wish to capture—larger lookback periods detect larger trends with fewer false signals, while smaller periods detect smaller trends with higher sensitivity.
